overview: Build a Python tool for authorized QA signup-flow automation on staging/dev using Selenium, with JSON reports and failure screenshots.
goals: Automate happy-path and negative signup scenarios in authorized environments.
Capture screenshots for failed steps and include paths in reports.
Provide a CLI-friendly runner for local and CI execution.
nonGoals: Bypassing anti-bot systems or account abuse automation.
constraints: Execution limited to authorized staging/dev targets.
assumptions: The target signup page and selectors are controlled by the QA team.
A browser and matching driver are available in runtime environments.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one happy-path scenario runs through CLI.
A JSON report is written on every run.
Failure screenshots are produced when possible and referenced by report entries.
Exit code is non-zero when at least one scenario fails.

stories.title: Generate PRD and project guardrails
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a clear PRD and build plan so implementation follows explicit QA-focused goals.
acceptanceCriteria: A RALPH PRD document exists and covers goals, constraints, and acceptance criteria.
A build plan exists and explains setup, config, and execution flow.
Documentation explicitly restricts usage to authorized environments.

stories.title: Implement Selenium runner scaffold
stories.description: As a QA engineer, I want a Python Selenium scaffold using page objects and config so scenarios are maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: A Python package exists under src with configuration, driver factory, page objects, and runner.
Runner attempts a signup scenario and writes structured JSON report output.
On scenario failure, report captures error message and optional screenshot path.

stories.title: Add baseline test coverage
stories.description: As a developer, I want baseline unit tests to validate config parsing and report serialization.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests validate boolean env parsing behavior.
Tests validate report write format and essential keys.
Test suite runs with pytest and exits success in CI-compatible mode.
